creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.
contentSnippet: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.
contentSnippet: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

title: (No AI) Anthropic’s $1.5 billion book piracy settlement approved by judge
author: Emma Roth
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/STK269_ANTHROPIC_2_A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

A federal judge has signed off on Anthropic's $1.5 billion class action settlement with authors who accused the company of training its AI models on copyrighted books, [as reported earlier by _Reuters_](https://www.reuters.com/world/us-judge-approves-anthropics-15-billion-settlement-copyright-lawsuit-2026-07-20/). In [an order on Monday](https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/28503369-anthropic-settlement-final-approval/), Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín writes that the settlement will provide "meaningful relief," offering authors around $3,000 for each book allegedly pirated by Anthropic.

The $1.5 billion settlement is the "largest known copyright recovery in history," according to [the law firm representing the plaintiffs](https://www.susmangodfrey.com/wins/court-grants-final-approval-of-historic-1-5-billion-ai-copyright-settlement/). A group of authors - Andrea Bartz, Charles Graeber, and Kirk Wallace Johnson - first filed a copyright lawsuit against A …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/968724/anthropic-authors-settlement-ai-copyright-approved)
contentSnippet:
A federal judge has signed off on Anthropic's $1.5 billion class action settlement with authors who accused the company of training its AI models on copyrighted books, as reported earlier by Reuters. In an order on Monday, Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín writes that the settlement will provide "meaningful relief," offering authors around $3,000 for each book allegedly pirated by Anthropic.
The $1.5 billion settlement is the "largest known copyright recovery in history," according to the law firm representing the plaintiffs. A group of authors - Andrea Bartz, Charles Graeber, and Kirk Wallace Johnson - first filed a copyright lawsuit against A …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: A federal judge has signed off on Anthropic's $1.5 billion class action settlement with authors who accused the company of training its AI models on copyrighted books, as reported earlier by Reuters. In an order on Monday, Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín writes that the settlement will provide "meaningful relief," offering authors around $3,000 for each book […]

title: (No AI) Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like Mythos
author: Emma Roth
content:
![Vector illustration of the Gemini logo.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/01/STK255_Google_Gemini_D.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google is launching [Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/gemini-models/gemini-3-6-flash-3-5-flash-lite-3-5-flash-cyber/) a new security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In [a blog post on Tuesday](https://deepmind.google/blog/introducing-gemini-3-5-flash-cyber/), Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber as a "cost-efficient and highly capable alternative" to larger, more expensive AI systems, such as the one offered by Anthropic's Mythos.

The cybersecurity model is built upon Gemini 3.5 Flash and will be available first to governments and trusted partners via CodeMender, [Google's security-focused coding agent](https://www.theverge.com/tech/933921/google-wants-to-compete-with-anthropics-mythos). As noted by Google, CodeMender can call upon 3.5 Flash Cyber "multiple times at high speed and low cost," allowing the AI agents to …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/968572/google-gemini-flash-cyber-ai-security-model)

Google is launching Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside a new security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In a blog post on Tuesday, Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber as a "cost-efficient and highly capable alternative" to larger, more expensive AI systems, such as the one offered by Anthropic's Mythos.
The cybersecurity model is built upon Gemini 3.5 Flash and will be available first to governments and trusted partners via CodeMender, Google's security-focused coding agent. As noted by Google, CodeMender can call upon 3.5 Flash Cyber "multiple times at high speed and low cost," allowing the AI agents to  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The cybersecurity model […]

creator: Lily Hay Newman
title: (No AI) A Sneaky Hacking Tool Targeting AI Infrastructure Is Lurking in Victims’ Blind Spots
dc:creator: Lily Hay Newman
content: A new type of malware can worm deep into AI coding systems to steal data and logins—and can flip a “death switch” to destroy files and keep out real users.
contentSnippet: A new type of malware can worm deep into AI coding systems to steal data and logins—and can flip a “death switch” to destroy files and keep out real users.

creator: Lauren Goode
title: (No AI) Nvidia Wants to Own Every Chip Inside AI Data Centers
dc:creator: Lauren Goode
content: Nvidia’s Vera Rubin platform combines CPUs and GPUs into a single system, reflecting the company’s growing ambition to power every layer of AI infrastructure.
contentSnippet: Nvidia’s Vera Rubin platform combines CPUs and GPUs into a single system, reflecting the company’s growing ambition to power every layer of AI infrastructure.
